data.title: Generational Group facilitated sale of Maffey’s Security Group to Guardian Fire Protection Services. Acquired in March 2025. Based in Elizabeth, NJ, Maffey's has 110 years of experience in security and locksmith services.
data.description: A prominent mergers and acquisitions advisory firm, Generational Group, recently facilitated the successful acquisition of Maffey’s Security Group by Guardian Fire Protection Services. The acquisition was finalized on March 31, 2025. Maffey’s Security Group, headquartered in Elizabeth, New Jersey, is a longstanding family-owned company with a rich history spanning over 110 years in the security and locksmith industry. The company offers specialized services in electronic and mechanical security systems.

data.title: Final chance to own luxury townhomes at The Brix in Orlando, Florida. Urban designs in prime location.
data.description: A high-end residential community is now providing luxury townhomes that feature urban-inspired designs. Located in a highly sought-after area, this exclusive community offers top-notch amenities and exquisite finishes throughout the homes. The development comprises a limited number of townhomes, with prices starting at $X,XXX,XXX. Designed for comfort and style, these townhomes boast spacious layouts, high-end appliances, and designer touches. Residents can enjoy the convenience of living in a prime location, surrounded by shopping, dining, and entertainment options. Don't miss the opportunity to own a luxury townhome in this exclusive community.

data.title: U.S. economy grows by 4.3% in Q3, beats expectations. Traders predict slowdown; GDP was 3.8% in Q2. SPY ETF near record high.
data.description: The U.S. economy had a strong third quarter in 2025, with the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growing at an impressive annualized pace of 4.3%. This exceeded expectations of 3.3% and was an acceleration from the second quarter's 3.8% growth. The SPY ETF is trading near record highs, reflecting the positive economic performance. However, traders are anticipating a cooling trend in growth, with prediction markets raising bets on slower growth in the fourth quarter and beyond. This reaction is based on historical trends, as blockbuster quarters are often not sustainable. In the past, strong GDP quarters have been followed by weaker growth in subsequent periods. For example, after a 4.7% growth in the third quarter of 2023, growth slowed to 3.4% in the fourth quarter and then dropped to 0.8%. This pattern has led to caution among traders despite the recent strong GDP data.

data.title: DALBAR awards financial industry leaders for outstanding customer service. Recognizing firms setting new standards for client care.
data.description: Setting new standards for exceptional customer service and leading the way in shaping the future of customer care, a recent event held in Marlborough, Massachusetts on December 23, 2025, highlighted the 2025 DALBAR Service Awards. These awards recognize financial firms that are dedicated to building lasting client connections and are actively preparing for the challenges and opportunities of the future. The event celebrated the achievements of firms in the industry that prioritize customer service excellence and innovation, showcasing their commitment to enhancing the overall client experience.

data.title: Exscape Group expands into Columbus, Ohio with 3 acquisitions - WinnScapes, Pony Lawncare, Shearer Patio & Landscape.
data.description: In a recent development on December 23, 2025, three prominent companies have decided to merge with Exscape Group - WinnScapes, Pony Lawncare and Landscaping, and Shearer Patio & Landscape Services / Polaris Pools. Exscape Group, recognized as a top-tier enterprise specializing in landscape maintenance, design, installation, and snow removal services, benefits from the support of BHMS Investments. This notable merger marks a significant transition in the industry landscape, with a focus on enhancing services and expanding market presence through combined efforts.

data.title: 2025 IPO market sees 202 initial offerings raising $44 billion, largest since 2021, led by unicorns.
data.description: In 2025, a total of 202 initial public offerings generated a record-breaking sum of $44 billion in funds raised. This marks the most significant performance in the IPO market since 2021, showcasing the remarkable success of unicorn companies that contributed to this milestone.

data.title: US CAN STARTUP founder Shun Ma expands into North American deep tech with investments in Kalino Bio and AllScale.io, boosting AI and Biotech ventures in Vancouver and Silicon Valley.
data.description: A previous recipient of the "Top 10 International Youth Maker" award is leveraging their expertise in cross-border incubation to drive the growth of AI, Biotech, and Fintech ventures in Vancouver and Silicon Valley. The initiative, led by US CAN STARTUP, a prominent cross-border incubation platform, aims to accelerate innovative ventures in these industries. The collaboration between Vancouver, BC, and Palo Alto, Calif., officially commenced on December 23, 2025, indicating a strategic move to foster entrepreneurship and development in key sectors.

data.title: Dow Jones rises on surprise Q3 GDP data; Novo stock soars on FDA approval for weight loss pill. Nasdaq leads with Solar stock dip.
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones index experienced a notable increase following the unexpected Q3 GDP data, with Novo stock seeing a significant surge after receiving FDA approval for a weight loss pill. The Nasdaq led the market after the surprising GDP results, while a solar stock took a significant dip. Live coverage of these market movements was provided by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Bitcoin dropped $40,000 from $126,000 due to whales panic-selling at $84,000, creating sustained selling pressure.
data.description: Bitcoin's value has plummeted by $40,000 since the beginning of October, with analysis pointing to whales as the culprits rather than retail traders or overleveraged longs. These large holders purchased near the all-time high of $126,000 and panic sold when the price hit $84,000, resulting in significant losses. The massive sell-off triggered a 32% decrease in Bitcoin's value. This information can be found in more detail on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Americans see 26% hike in ACA marketplace premiums; many turn to healthsharing like Liberty HealthShare. Inquiries growing.
data.description: Premiums for ACA marketplace plans have surged by 26%, leaving many Americans struggling to afford coverage. As a result, thousands are turning to healthsharing programs as a more cost-effective option. Liberty HealthShare has reported a significant uptick in inquiries and enrollments. The rise in premiums has prompted individuals to seek alternative and more affordable avenues for healthcare coverage. This shift highlights the increasing financial burden placed on individuals by traditional health insurance plans.

data.title: Cipher expands into US wholesale power market through Ohio data center purchase, enhancing Bitcoin mining capabilities.
data.description: Cipher, a Bitcoin mining company, has acquired a site in the largest wholesale power market in the US, in a strategic move to expand its infrastructure. This acquisition is a significant step for Cipher as they aim to enhance their presence in the market. This move comes at a time when miners are increasingly diversifying their infrastructure strategies to stay competitive. The acquisition is a part of Cipher's proactive approach to position themselves in a key market, tapping into the potential for growth. This development highlights the company's commitment to scaling up its operations and increasing its market share.
